mechanism_narrative: Monthly INDPRO momentum is an always-on cyclical signal: higher 3-month growth in industrial production increases expected S&P 500 returns because improving real output raises near-term revenue revisions for cyclical firms.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value for 2021-12 was 101.8926 as of the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth does not produce positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation crosses into a high-inflation zone while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, equity discount rates reprice upward before policy rates move, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change crosses above 5.0% while FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2021-12 was 280.192 and FEDFUNDS was 0.08
regime_hint: inflation shock with policy rate behind the curve
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE combined with rapid PAYEMS gains signals labor-market overheating; the resulting wage and policy-tightening pressure reduces equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0% and PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 500 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2021-12 was 3.9 and PAYEMS was 148951
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor supply
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term for low UNRATE and strong PAYEMS has a nonnegative coefficient for 60-day forward returns in walk-forward tests across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in RSAFS signals slowing household goods demand and weaker near-term earnings momentum for consumer-facing firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS downside-trigger months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 65% of cases or by returns above the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above their expansion threshold indicate strong construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household formation, which supports cyclical earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST readings above 1600 thousand units are not followed by higher 60-day returns than readings below that threshold in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth is an always-on real-demand signal: stronger quarterly real GDP growth raises aggregate cash-flow expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of quarterly GDPC1 growth has no positive association with 60-day S&P 500 returns and the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.02 for three straight years.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in FEDFUNDS marks the start of realized monetary tightening; higher short rates raise discount factors and reduce liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 1-month change crosses above 0.10 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS upside breaks are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than the sample base rate or if mean triggered returns are nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL signals a supply-side recovery that improves real output while easing margin and discount-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth crosses above 0 while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses below its prior 6-month rate
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and CPIAUCSL monthly vintages were available in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either INDPRO-only or CPIAUCSL-only signals for 20-day returns in walk-forward tests, or if its hit rate is below 50%.
